title: userns: support --userns=auto
body:
automatically pick an empty range and create an user namespace for the                                                  container.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
The Used ranges of UIDs has to be recorded inside of containers/storage.  Is that happening? 
The maximum range has to be allocated from /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid.
My idea would be to have podman/CRI-o/Buildah installs reserve containers in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid 

cat /etc/subuid
3267:100000:65536
ashleycui:200000:65536
containers:2147483646:2147483648


Then container storage would use from this range.
We have to guarantee that each container gets a different range then every other container created in container storage.
Also I would like to see us have tools in container/storage to figure out the minimum size of uids, by both getting the max UID in the storage layers, as well as reading /etc/passwd?

> The Used ranges of UIDs has to be recorded inside of containers/storage. Is that happening?

I'll need to move the logic down to containers/storage.  It is not currently done.



> cat /etc/subuid
> 3267:100000:65536
> ashleycui:200000:65536
> containers:2147483646:2147483648

that means we create a new user "containers"?  Shouldn't it be assigned to root?

```
root:2147483646:2147483648
```

No I think it should be just on containers, or maybe we put an invalid users name, so a user named containers could still be added.

"container auto ranges"

Bottom line we need to make sure useradd and systemd do not overlay with the range we have allocated.
